source_intake_record.raw_question_text: Find the momentum flux density of TEM waves in a coaxial transmission line of inner radius $a$ and outer radius $b$.
source_intake_record.raw_answer_text: $\nabla \cdot \overparen{T}=\frac{2 \epsilon_{o} A^{2} \omega}{c\left(x^{2}+y^{2}\right)} \cos (k z-\omega t) \sin (k z-\omega t) \hat{z}=\frac{\partial}{\partial t}\left(\vec{p}_{\mathrm{em}}\right)$

candidate_problem_record.raw_question_text: Find the momentum flux density of TEM waves in a coaxial transmission line of inner radius $a$ and outer radius $b$.
candidate_problem_record.raw_answer_text: $\nabla \cdot \overparen{T}=\frac{2 \epsilon_{o} A^{2} \omega}{c\left(x^{2}+y^{2}\right)} \cos (k z-\omega t) \sin (k z-\omega t) \hat{z}=\frac{\partial}{\partial t}\left(\vec{p}_{\mathrm{em}}\right)$
candidate_problem_record.multi_solution_policy_rationale: 该数据集保留多解潜力评估，但不默认强推多解 agent。

extraction_notes: images field contains a PIL image object string representation rather than a file path or URL
metadata.reasoning_chain:
Inside the transmission line, for a constant $A$, we have
\[
\vec{E}=\frac{A \cos (k z-\omega t)}{s} \hat{s}
\]
and
\[
\vec{B}=\frac{A \cos (k z-\omega t)}{c s} \hat{\phi}
\]
To find the momentum flux density, we need to find $-\overparen{T}$ which requires finding the components of $\vec{T}$. We can express $\vec{E}$ and $\vec{B}$ in terms of Cartesian coordinates using
\[
\begin{array}{l}
s=\sqrt{x^{2}+y^{2}} \\
\hat{s}=\cos \phi \hat{x}+\sin \phi \hat{y}
\end{array}
\]
and
\[
\hat{\phi}=-\sin \phi \hat{x}+\cos \phi \hat{y}
\]

Thus,
\[
\vec{E}=\frac{A \cos (k z-\omega t)}{\sqrt{x^{2}+y^{2}}}(\cos \phi \hat{x}+\sin \phi \hat{y})
\]
and
\[
\vec{B}=\frac{A \cos (k z-\omega t)}{c \sqrt{x^{2}+y^{2}}}(-\sin \phi \hat{x}+\cos \phi \hat{y})
\]

We can use these, along with
\[
T_{i j}=\epsilon_{o}\left(E_{i} E_{j}-\frac{1}{2} \delta_{i j} E^{2}\right)+\frac{1}{\mu_{o}}\left(B_{i} B_{j}-\frac{1}{2} \delta_{i j} B^{2}\right)
\]
to solve for the components of $\overparen{T}$.
\[
\begin{aligned}
T_{x x}= & \frac{1}{2} \epsilon_{o}\left(E_{x}^{2}-E_{y}^{2}-E_{z}^{2}\right)+\frac{1}{2 \mu_{o}}\left(B_{x}^{2}-B_{y}^{2}-B_{z}^{2}\right) \\
= & \frac{1}{2} \epsilon_{o}\left(\frac{A \cos (k z-\omega t)}{\sqrt{x^{2}+y^{2}}}\right)^{2}\left(\cos ^{2} \phi-\sin ^{2} \phi\right) \\
& +\frac{1}{2 \mu_{o}}\left(\frac{A \cos (k z-\omega t)}{c \sqrt{x^{2}+y^{2}}}\right)^{2}\left(\sin ^{2} \phi-\cos ^{2} \phi\right) \\
= & \frac{\epsilon_{o} A^{2} \cos ^{2}(k z-\omega t)}{2\left(x^{2}+y^{2}\right)}\left(\cos ^{2} \phi-\sin ^{2} \phi\right)+\frac{A^{2} \cos ^{2}(k z-\omega t)}{2 \mu_{o} c^{2}\left(x^{2}+y^{2}\right)}\left(\sin ^{2} \phi-\cos ^{2} \phi\right)
\end{aligned}
\]

Recalling $\frac{1}{c^{2}}=\mu_{o} \epsilon_{o}$,
\[
T_{x x}=\frac{\epsilon_{o} A^{2} \cos ^{2}(k z-\omega t)}{2\left(x^{2}+y^{2}\right)}\left(\cos ^{2} \phi-\sin ^{2} \phi+\sin ^{2} \phi-\cos ^{2} \phi\right)=0
\]
\[
\begin{array}{l}
T_{x y}=\epsilon_{o} E_{x} E_{y}+\frac{1}{\mu_{o}} B_{x} B_{y} \\
=\epsilon_{o}\left(\frac{A \cos (k z-\omega t)}{\sqrt{x^{2}+y^{2}}}\right)^{2} \cos \phi \sin \phi-\frac{1}{\mu_{o}}\left(\frac{A \cos (k z-\omega t)}{c \sqrt{x^{2}+y^{2}}}\right)^{2} \cos \phi \sin \phi \\
=\epsilon_{\rho}\left(\frac{A \cos (k z-\omega t)}{\sqrt{x^{2}+y^{2}}}\right)^{2}(\cos \phi \sin \phi-\cos \phi \sin \phi)=0 \\
T_{x z}=\epsilon_{o} E_{x} E_{z}+\frac{1}{\mu_{o}} B_{x} B_{z}=0 \\
T_{y x}=T_{x y}=0 \\
T_{y y}=\frac{1}{2} \epsilon_{o}\left(E_{y}^{2}-E_{x}^{2}-E_{z}^{2}\right)+\frac{1}{2 \mu_{o}}\left(B_{y}^{2}-B_{x}^{2}-B_{z}^{2}\right) \\
=T_{x x}+\epsilon_{o}\left(E_{x}^{2}-E_{y}^{2}\right)+\frac{1}{\mu_{o}}\left(B_{x}^{2}-B_{y}^{2}\right) \\
=\epsilon_{o}\left[E_{x}^{2}-E_{y}^{2}+c^{2}\left(B_{x}^{2}-B_{y}^{2}\right)\right] \\
=\epsilon_{o}\left[\left(\frac{A^{2} \cos ^{2}(k z-\omega t)}{x^{2}+y^{2}}\right)\left(\cos ^{2} \phi-\sin ^{2} \phi\right)\right. \\
\left.+c^{2}\left(\frac{A^{2} \cos ^{2}(k z-\omega t)}{c^{2}\left(x^{2}+y^{2}\right)}\right)\left(\sin ^{2} \phi-\cos ^{2} \phi\right)\right]=0 \\
T_{y z}=\epsilon_{o} E_{y} E_{z}+\frac{1}{\mu_{o}} B_{y} B_{z}=0 \\
T_{z x}=T_{x z}=0 \\
T_{z y}=T_{y z}=0
\end{array}
\]
\[
\begin{aligned}
T_{y y} & =\frac{1}{2} \epsilon_{o}\left(E_{z}^{2}-E_{x}^{2}-E_{y}^{2}\right)+\frac{1}{2 \mu_{o}}\left(B_{z}^{2}-B_{x}^{2}-B_{y}^{2}\right) \\
& =-\frac{1}{2}\left[\epsilon_{o}\left(E_{x}^{2}+E_{y}^{2}\right)+\frac{1}{\mu_{o}}\left(B_{x}^{2}+B_{y}^{2}\right)\right] \\
& =-\frac{1}{2} \epsilon_{o}\left[\left(\frac{A^{2} \cos ^{2}(k z-\omega t)}{x^{2}+y^{2}}\right)\left(\cos ^{2} \phi+\sin ^{2} \phi\right)+c^{2}\left(\frac{A^{2} \cos ^{2}(k z-\omega t)}{c^{2}\left(x^{2}+y^{2}\right)}\right)\left(\sin ^{2} \phi+\cos ^{2} \phi\right)\right] \\
& =-\frac{\epsilon_{o} A^{2} \cos ^{2}(k z-\omega t)}{x^{2}+y^{2}}
\end{aligned}
\]

Therefore, the momentum flux density is given by
\[
-\overparen{T}=-\left(\begin{array}{ccc}
0 & 0 & 0 \\
0 & 0 & 0 \\
0 & 0 & -\frac{\epsilon_{o} A^{2} \cos ^{2}(k z-\omega t)}{x^{2}+y^{2}}
\end{array}\right)=\left(\begin{array}{ccc}
0 & 0 & 0 \\
0 & 0 & 0 \\
0 & 0 & \frac{\epsilon_{o} A^{2} \cos ^{2}(k z-\omega t)}{x^{2}+y^{2}}
\end{array}\right)
\]

We can check this by considering
\[
\frac{\partial}{\partial t}\left(\vec{p}_{\mathrm{em}}\right)=\nabla \cdot \vec{T}
\]
where
\[
\overrightarrow{\mathcal{P}}_{\mathrm{e} \mathrm{~m}}=\mu_{o} \epsilon_{o} \vec{S}=\epsilon_{o}(\vec{E} \times \vec{B})=\frac{\epsilon_{o} A^{2} \cos ^{2}(k z-\omega t)}{c s^{2}} \hat{z}
\]

So
\[
\begin{aligned}
\frac{\partial}{\partial t}\left(\vec{p}_{\mathrm{em}}\right) & =\frac{\epsilon_{o} A^{2}}{c s^{2}} 2 \cos (k z-\omega t)(-\sin (k z-\omega t))(-\omega) \hat{z} \\
& =\frac{2 \epsilon_{o} A^{2} \omega}{c s^{2}} \cos (k z-\omega t) \sin (k z-\omega t) \hat{z} \\
& =\frac{2 \epsilon_{o} A^{2} \omega}{c\left(x^{2}+y^{2}\right)} \cos (k z-\omega t) \sin (k z-\omega t) \hat{z}
\end{aligned}
\]

Now
\[
\begin{array}{l}
\nabla \cdot \overparen{T}=\left(\begin{array}{lll}
\frac{\partial}{\partial x} & \frac{\partial}{\partial y} & \frac{\partial}{\partial z}
\end{array}\right)\left(\begin{array}{ccc}
0 & 0 & 0 \\
0 & 0 & 0 \\
0 & 0 & -\frac{\epsilon_{o} A^{2} \cos ^{2}(k z-\omega t)}{x^{2}+y^{2}}
\end{array}\right) \\
=\left(\begin{array}{ccc}
0 & 0 & \left.\frac{\epsilon_{o} A^{2}}{x^{2}+y^{2}}(-2 \cos (k z-\omega t))(-\sin (k z-\omega t))(k)\right)
\end{array}\right. \\
=\frac{\epsilon_{o} A^{2}}{x^{2}+y^{2}}(-2 \cos (k z-\omega t))(-\sin (k z-\omega t))(k) \hat{z} \\
=\frac{2 \epsilon_{o} A^{2} k}{x^{2}+y^{2}} \cos (k z-\omega t) \sin (k z-\omega t) \hat{z}
\end{array}
\]

Using $k=\omega / c$,
\[
\nabla \cdot \overparen{T}=\frac{2 \epsilon_{o} A^{2} \omega}{c\left(x^{2}+y^{2}\right)} \cos (k z-\omega t) \sin (k z-\omega t) \hat{z}=\frac{\partial}{\partial t}\left(\vec{p}_{\mathrm{em}}\right)
\]
as expected.

answer_slots.target_text: Find the momentum flux density of TEM waves in a coaxial transmission line of inner radius $a$ and outer radius $b$.
answer_slots.expected_answer: $\nabla \cdot \overparen{T}=\frac{2 \epsilon_{o} A^{2} \omega}{c\left(x^{2}+y^{2}\right)} \cos (k z-\omega t) \sin (k z-\omega t) \hat{z}=\frac{\partial}{\partial t}\left(\vec{p}_{\mathrm{em}}\right)$
